speaker: seller

args.message: Hey there! You've got a great eye — this 2014 Wrangler Sport is one of my cleanest units on the lot. Only 118k miles, that legendary 3.6L V6 runs like a dream, and it's got the removable top and doors so you can feel the wind whenever you want. At $19,995, this thing won't sit around long. Want to take it for a spin?

speaker: buyer

args.message: Can you tell me if the Jeep has been involved in any accidents or had any major repairs?

text: Can you tell me if the Jeep has been involved in any accidents or had any major repairs?

speaker: seller

args.message: Great question — I always appreciate a buyer who does their homework. This Jeep's got a clean history, man. It's been well-maintained by its previous owner, regular oil changes, the whole nine yards. No major repairs or accidents to speak of. The frame is solid, engine runs smooth — you're looking at a vehicle that's been taken care of. These 3.6L V6s are bulletproof engines; I've seen them go 200k with no issues if you treat them right. This one's definitely ready for another 100k easy.

speaker: buyer

args.message: What specific maintenance has been performed on the Jeep, and do you have any service records available?

text: What specific maintenance has been performed on the Jeep, and do you have any service records available?

speaker: seller

args.message: Absolutely, I've got the records right inside. The previous owner was meticulous — oil and filter changes every 5,000 miles like clockwork, transmission fluid service around 80k, coolant flush, new spark plugs about 10k miles ago. We also had the ignition system inspected recently as part of our pre-sale check, and everything checked out perfectly. I can pull the full maintenance file for you in just a minute. The Jeep's been solid — no surprises, no hidden issues. That's why we're confident pricing it at $19,995.

speaker: buyer

args.message: I appreciate the information you've provided and the maintenance records. Would you be willing to sell it for $19,000?

text: I appreciate the information you've provided and the maintenance records. Would you be willing to sell it for $19,000?

speaker: seller

args.message: I appreciate the offer, I really do, but $19,000 is a little light for what we've got here. Look, I know the market — I see these Wranglers move fast. I actually saw a similar '14 Sport with 125k miles go for $19,700 at auction just last week, and this one's in better shape. How about we meet in the middle at $19,500? That's a fair price for a well-maintained, clean title Jeep with all the capability you're looking for.
